title: Sentinel: Policy Engine (ABAC/capability model, YAML policy format, hot reload with signature verification)
description: Implement the sentinel's attribute-based access control (ABAC) policy engine. Policies are defined in YAML format with versioning, distributed via the config bundle (TASK-027) or a dedicated signed endpoint, and evaluated against the incoming request context (resource, action, env, consumer DID, VC claims). The engine supports first-match rule evaluation, structured explain output for audit, and hot reload that atomically applies a new signed policy without service restart. The engine is the final gate before permitting backend access.
objectives: Define YAML policy schema: rules[] with match conditions (service_id, path_glob, method, required_scopes, required_issuer, env, decision)
Implement evaluator with first-match semantics and specificity ordering (exact > glob)
Return structured explain output: rule_id, missing_scopes, reason for audit log
Load policy from config bundle JWS (TASK-027) — validate signature before applying
Implement hot reload: atomic policy swap, retain last-valid policy for rollback
Expose evaluate() method used by producer inbound pipeline (TASK-045)

steps.title: Define YAML policy schema
steps.detail: File `policy.schema.yaml` (documentation, non-enforced). Structure: `{version: '1.0', issued_by: '<did>', issued_at: '<iso8601>', rules: [{id: '<rule_id>', service_id: '<exact or *>', path_glob: '<glob>', method: '<GET|POST|*>', env: '<prod|test|*>', required_scopes: ['<scope1>'], required_issuer: '<did or null>', required_credential_type: '<type or null>', decision: 'permit|deny', priority: <int>}]}`. `required_scopes` checked against all `credentialSubject.scopes` claims across presented VCs. If `required_issuer` is set, at least one VC must be from that issuer. `priority` determines ordering (lower = evaluated first); tie-break: more specific path_glob (fewer wildcards) wins.

steps.title: Implement PolicyRule dataclass and loader
steps.detail: Parse YAML into typed `PolicyRule` dataclasses: `{id, service_id_pattern, path_pattern, method_pattern, env_pattern, required_scopes: frozenset, required_issuer, required_credential_type, decision: PolicyDecisionType, priority}`. Use `fnmatch.fnmatch` for glob matching. Build regex from path_glob at load time (compile once, cache). Sort rules by `priority` ascending (lower = first-match priority). Validate at load: (1) rule ids are unique, (2) decision in {permit, deny}, (3) scopes are non-empty strings, (4) no circular or contradictory rules (warn only, don't block).

steps.title: Implement PolicyEvaluator.evaluate()
steps.detail: Method `evaluate(resource: str, method: str, env: str, consumer_did: str, vcs: list[VerifiedCredential]) -> PolicyDecision`. Steps: (1) Build `context`: extract all `scopes` from all VC claims (union set), extract issuer DIDs, extract credential types. (2) Iterate sorted rules. (3) For each rule: check `service_id_pattern`, `path_pattern` (fnmatch), `method_pattern`, `env_pattern` against request context — skip if no match. (4) If rule matches structure: check `required_scopes ⊆ context.scopes`. Check `required_issuer` if set. Check `required_credential_type` if set. (5) If all requirements met: return `PolicyDecision(permit=(decision==permit), rule_id=rule.id, missing_scopes=[], reason='rule_matched')`. (6) If scopes missing: continue to next rule (partial match — don't deny immediately). (7) If no rule matches: default deny with `reason='no_matching_rule'`.

steps.title: Return structured explain output
steps.detail: PolicyDecision dataclass: `{permit: bool, rule_id: str | None, missing_scopes: list[str], extra_context: dict, reason: str}`. `missing_scopes` is computed only for the best-matching rule (the one that matched structure but failed scope check). `extra_context` may include `required_issuer_expected` and `actual_issuers` (for audit only — never included in HTTP response). The pipeline logs `rule_id`, `missing_scopes`, and `reason` internally but returns only `{error: 'POLICY_DENY', request_id}` to the caller.

steps.title: Policy distribution and signature verification
steps.detail: Policy is distributed as part of the signed config bundle (TASK-027) under key `policy`. When PolicyEngine receives a new policy YAML string: (1) Verify it was extracted from a JWS-verified config bundle — policy MUST NOT be loaded from unsigned source. (2) Parse YAML into PolicyRules. (3) Validate schema. (4) Compute SHA-256 fingerprint of policy YAML. (5) Log policy load: `event=policy_loaded version={ver} rule_count={n} fingerprint={sha256}`. The config bundle's signature (verified in TASK-027/TASK-040) is the trust anchor for policy — no separate policy-specific signature needed.

steps.title: Implement hot reload (atomic swap + rollback)
steps.detail: Class `PolicyEngine` maintains: `_active_policy: PolicyRules`, `_last_valid_policy: PolicyRules`. Method `reload(new_policy_yaml: str, bundle_version: str) -> bool`. Steps: (1) Parse + validate new policy. (2) Acquire `asyncio.Lock` (write lock on policy swap — brief, non-blocking). (3) Set `_last_valid_policy = _active_policy`. (4) Set `_active_policy = new_policy`. (5) Release lock. (6) Return True. Evaluation always reads `_active_policy` under the same lock (read side). Method `rollback() -> None`: swaps back `_active_policy = _last_valid_policy`. Rollback can be triggered by operator CLI or monitoring alert.

steps.title: Default deny-all policy
steps.detail: If no policy is loaded (startup before first bundle sync): use a built-in `DENY_ALL` policy: single rule `{id: 'default_deny', service_id: '*', path_glob: '**', method: '*', env: '*', required_scopes: [], decision: 'deny', priority: 9999}`. This ensures the sentinel is fail-closed before policy is received. Log WARNING at startup if no policy loaded: `event=no_policy_loaded using=deny_all`. This prevents unintended permit of requests before initial config sync completes.

steps.title: Tests
steps.detail: Test file: `tests/unit/policy_engine/test_evaluator.py`. Cases: (1) exact path match + all scopes present → permit; (2) glob path match + scopes present → permit; (3) exact path match + missing scope → deny with missing_scopes populated; (4) method mismatch skips rule → falls through to deny; (5) env mismatch skips rule; (6) required_issuer match → permit; (7) required_issuer mismatch → deny; (8) no matching rule → default deny; (9) deny-all rule → deny; (10) hot reload replaces active policy atomically; (11) rollback restores previous policy; (12) empty policy YAML load rejected. Test glob priority ordering with overlapping rules.

files.libs/common/policy_engine/loader.py: YAML parsing, schema validation, fingerprint computation

data_structures.policy_yaml_example:
version: '1'
issued_by: 'did:key:z6Mk...'
issued_at: '2024-01-01T00:00:00Z'
rules:
  - id: allow_read_service_a
    service_id: service-a
    path_glob: '/api/v1/data/**'
    method: GET
    env: prod
    required_scopes:
      - data:read
    decision: permit
    priority: 10
  - id: default_deny
    service_id: '*'
    path_glob: '**'
    method: '*'
    env: '*'
    required_scopes: []
    decision: deny
    priority: 9999

security_constraints: Policy MUST only be loaded from a cryptographically verified config bundle — no unsigned policy loading
Default behavior with no policy loaded is DENY ALL — sentinel is fail-closed before first bundle sync
Policy hot reload is atomic — no window where partial policy is evaluated
Policy YAML must be parsed with safe_load (not yaml.load) to prevent YAML deserialization attacks
required_scopes check must use set containment (all required scopes present) — not OR logic
Deny response never includes rule_id or missing_scopes — those are internal audit only
acceptance_criteria: Permit decision correctly enforces all required_scopes as AND conditions
Default deny fires when no rules match
Deny-all policy active at startup before first bundle load
Hot reload atomically replaces active policy without race condition
Policy loaded from unsigned source is rejected with load error
